positionData.text: The U-SU is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) funded by student fees and governed by the Board of Directors composed of 15 voting members, 8 of whom are student directors.

The Board of Directors (BOD) has 8 student leaders and 8 faculty, staff, administrator, and alumni members who help shape policy, structure, and advocate for the U-SU across campus. The BOD is responsible for all financial & legal responsibility of running a non-profit organization.
The Chair and Vice Chair will be elected during the annual election process. The candidates with the highest number of votes for the position will be the Chair and Vice Chair respectively.

list: Chair the monthly meetings of the Board of Directors
Co-chair the Personnel Committee and serve as a non-voting member of all other standing committees
Meet weekly with the Executive Director and Vice Chair to prepare meeting agenda and plan development opportunities for the student directors
Represent the U-SU Board of Directors in conferences, meetings, and events.
Ensure the Board’s initiatives align with the organization’s mission and values.

positionData.text: The U-SU is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) funded by student fees and governed by the Board of Directors composed of 15 voting members, 8 of whom are student directors.

The Board of Directors (BOD) has 8 student leaders and 8 faculty, staff, administrator, and alumni members who help shape policy, structure, and advocate for the U-SU across campus. The BOD is responsible for all financial & legal responsibility of running a non-profit organization.
The Chair and Vice Chair will be elected during the annual election process. The candidates with the highest number of votes for the position will be the Chair and Vice Chair respectively.
The Role of the Vice Chair

list: Attend all of the Board meetings and serve as a voting member
Perform the responsibilities of the Chair in the absence of the Chair

Assist the Chair in performing their duties, including planning for meetings and development opportunities to ensure Board actions and discussions remain aligned with the organization’s mission and values. Chair the ad hoc committees established by the Board of Directors
Meet weekly with the Executive Director and Chair to prepare meeting agendas plan development opportunities for the student directors

positionData.text: The U-SU is a nonprofit 501(c)(3) funded by student fees and governed by the Board of Directors composed of 15 voting members, 8 of whom are student directors.

The Board of Directors (BOD) has 8 student leaders and 8 faculty, staff, administrator, and alumni members who help shape policy, structure, and advocate for the U-SU across campus. The BOD is responsible for all financial & legal responsibility of running a non-profit organization.
The Chair and Vice Chair will be elected during the annual election process. The candidates with the highest number of votes for the position will be the Chair and Vice Chair respectively.
The Role of a Student Director

list: Attend all of the Board meetings and serve as a voting member
Represent students by actively seeking out student feedback and sharing their opinions on U-SU programs, services, and building use
Serve on sub-committees and report updates and recommendations back to the Board meetings
Serve as an ambassador for the U-SU, promoting its programs, services, involvement, policies and employment opportunities
